preFix.title: Fitting a sliding wardrobe door system
preFix.subtitle: Install a top-hung sliding door track inside an alcove or wardrobe opening
preFix.excerpt: How to fit a top-hung sliding door track system to a wardrobe opening or alcove, covering track alignment, fixing to the head of the opening, hanging the doors on their rollers, and adjusting the height and plumb of each door.
preFix.sourceNotes: City and Guilds Level 2 Site Carpentry. TRADA Wood Information Sheet (sliding door ironmongery). Cassell's Cyclopaedia of Mechanics.

content.text: system can be fitted to almost any wardrobe opening provided the head is level and solid. The doors bypass each other rather than swinging outward, so they work in tight rooms and against beds. Most systems use a double or triple track to allow two or three doors to overlap; measure the opening width against the track length before ordering, and choose a track deep enough (front-to-back) for the number of doors required.

Sliding door track kit (track, rollers, bottom guide channel, and fascia cover if included). Screws appropriate for the head material (into timber: 40 mm No. 8; into plasterboard head: use a solid timber batten fixed first). Wardrobe door panels cut to size (typically 18 mm MDF or veneered chipboard). End stops for the track.
Step 1, measure and cut the track

Measure the width of the opening between the two side walls or uprights. Cut the track to this length with a hacksaw or the tenon saw. Most aluminium tracks cut cleanly with fine teeth. Check which channel carries the front door and which carries the back door: the front channel sits further forward, the back channel is recessed. Mark the front edge of the track on the head of the opening with the carpenters pencil.
Step 2, fix the top track level

Hold the track against the head at the marked position. Check it is level along its full length with the spirit level. If the head is slightly out of level, pack one end with a thin timber shim. Fix through the pre-drilled holes in the track into the head at 300 mm centres maximum. Drive screws fully home; a loose track will allow the doors to rock and jump the rollers under use.

Fit the roller brackets to the top edge of each door at the positions specified in the track manufacturer's instructions (typically 50 mm in from each corner). Tilt each door and hook its rollers into the track channel from below, back door first, then front door. Most rollers click into the channel; they cannot be inserted once the end stop is fitted, so hang both doors before fitting the end stops. Check each door hangs plumb using the combination square held against the side edge. Adjust the height of each roller using the adjusting screw on the roller bracket until the door is plumb and the gap at the floor is even across the full width.
Step 4, fit the bottom guide and end stops

Cut the bottom guide channel to the same length as the track and fix it to the floor, centred under the top track. The bottom guide accepts a small plastic or nylon peg on the bottom of each door that keeps it from swinging outward without bearing any weight. Fix end stops at both ends of the top track to prevent the doors from running off the end. Slide each door across its full travel and check the gaps at top, bottom, and sides are consistent. Sand any edge that binds against the adjacent door or wall with the sanding block.

attrs.fix: Re-check that the bottom peg is engaged in the channel. If the door leans forward or back when centred, reposition the bottom channel to bring the door plumb.
attrs.cause: The bottom guide peg is not seated in the guide channel, or the channel is positioned too far forward or back.
attrs.symptom: Door swings inward or outward at the bottom

attrs.fix: Check each fixing screw for tightness. Run a finger along the underside of the track to feel for any bow or step at a fixing point; ease any overtightened screw by a quarter turn to relieve local distortion.
attrs.cause: The top track is not fixed firmly enough, or a screw is pulling the track slightly out of alignment at one fixing point.
attrs.symptom: Door jumps or skips in the track when slid

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
tricolon: tricolon (three parallel items) — two adjectives almost always beats three
paragraph reads at grade 11.3 (qc threshold: 11) — simplify (target grade 6-8)
initialFindings.snippet: A top-hung door track system can be fitted to almost any wardrobe opening provided the head is level and solid. The doors bypass each other
